core_mechanism: A linear self-excitation eigenvalue problem in which a positive feedback source term is balanced against a diffusion operator on a bounded domain, so that tuning a geometric control parameter drives a bifurcation where the dominant (leading) eigenmode swaps spatial symmetry and switches between a real eigenvalue (stationary state) and a complex-conjugate pair (oscillatory state), i.e. a mode-crossing / Hopf-type transition of the leading eigenmode.
brief_reason: The abstract describes a kinematic (linear) induction problem with a critical aspect ratio at which the dominant field structure switches from an equatorial to an axial dipole and where solutions change from steady to oscillatory, which is exactly a symmetry-swapping, stationary-to-oscillatory bifurcation of the leading eigenmode of a self-excitation-versus-diffusion operator.
